title: Translationally Covariant Modulated Symmetries: Classification and Goldstone
authors: Bo-Ting Chen, Zihan Zhou, Biao Lian
abstract: Modulated symmetries are global symmetries with a spatially dependent unit of charge, such as the dipole symmetry and the exponential symmetry. We give the generic condition for a modulated symmetry to be compatible with translationally symmetric Hamiltonians, which we define as a translationally covariant modulated symmetry (TCMS). For Abelian TCMSs, we prove that their units of charge can only contain multipole, exponential and harmonic components. Particularly, we classify all the one-dimensional TCMSs by real Jordan normal form blocks. We further derive the generic Goldstone action for SSB phases of continuous TCMSs, by which we show that a broken multipole symmetry gives higher-order gapless Goldstone modes, a broken harmonic symmetry gives gapless Goldstone modes at finite momenta, and a broken exponential symmetry gives no gapless Goldstone modes, modifying the conventional Goldstone theorem.
summary: This theoretical work classifies modulated global symmetries (TCMS) compatible with translationally invariant Hamiltonians. It derives the low-energy effective action for SSB based on the symmetry type (multipole, exponential, harmonic). The key finding is that breaking these symmetries leads to exotic Goldstone modes, modifying conventional theorems and predicting unique spectral features.
main_problem: To classify translationally covariant modulated symmetries (TCMS) and derive the universal effective Goldstone action for their spontaneous symmetry breaking (SSB).
main_result: The nature of the broken symmetry dictates the low-energy excitation spectrum: multipole yields higher-order dispersion, harmonic yields modes at finite momenta, and exponential yields no gapless mode.
method: The analysis uses algebraic classification based on commutation relations (Jordan normal form) and derives effective Lagrangians via coset construction techniques.
model_or_system: The study applies to quantum many-body systems, exemplified by 1D deformed Bose-Hubbard models exhibiting dipole, exponential, or harmonic modulated symmetries.
key_observables:
Goldstone mode dispersions ($\omega$ vs $k$), order parameters (e.g., $\langle\hat{b}^\dagger_j \hat{b}_{j+1}angle$), and the structure of the effective Lagrangian components ($M, W^{\mu
u}$).
important_parameters: The symmetry type is characterized by the structure constant matrix $A_\mu$, which dictates whether the symmetry is multipole, exponential, or harmonic.
main_assumptions: The analysis focuses on continuous Abelian TCMSs and often employs mean-field approximations or variational ansatzes for calculations.
figures_summary: Figures schematically show dispersion relations for different symmetries (e.g., quadratic dispersion for multipole), and tables summarize the resulting Goldstone Lagrangians.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by defining TCMSs, classifying them based on algebraic constraints (Jordan form), deriving the generic low-energy action using coset methods, and finally analyzing the specific consequences of SSB for each symmetry type.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful, systematic framework to analyze exotic gapless excitations in strongly correlated systems beyond standard symmetries, directly impacting understanding of quantum phases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
